evidence: Error handling entrypoints are split across multiple module boundaries: `handleError.ts` exports canonical wrappers and deprecated shims (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts:15-45`), while consumers in this batch still import lower-level reporters directly (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:3`, `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts:2`).

A shared UI singleton is created at import time (`toastManager = Toast.createToastManager()` in `src/shared/components/ui/toast.tsx:11`) and consumed by runtime facade exports (`src/shared/components/ui/runtime/sonner.tsx:2,25-33`), creating cross-module mutable state coupling.

Coordinator hooks/providers are acting as boundary hubs: `ProjectContext` composes auth + settings + selection + CRUD + defaults (`src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx:4-8,41-66`) and `useMediaGalleryHandlers` coordinates navigation, filter, lightbox, context state, and error reporting via a broad argument surface (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:16-44,116-123`).

Task builders are carrying very wide cross-concern parameter bags instead of focused contracts: `IndividualTravelSegmentParams` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` includes lineage, UI restoration, generation routing, structure-video migration, and worker payload fields in one interface (lines 36-96), then spreads through multiple assembly stages (`buildSegmentState`, `buildTaskParamsPayload`, `buildIndividualSegmentParamsPayload`).

`CanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` similarly mixes transport metadata, UI state, worker controls, lineage, and mode-specific fields, then requires manual normalization/validation gates (`normalizeJoinClipsParams`, `buildJoinClipsPayload`, lines 110-402).

Low-leverage adapter layers are accumulating around the same flows: `resolveUseSegmentSlotModeInput` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts` is a near-total field-forwarder (lines 127-151), and `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` includes legacy adapter wrappers that mostly translate result shapes (`handleAddToShotLegacyAdapter`, `handleCreateShotLegacyAdapter`, lines 335-358).

Task-creation dependency surface is split: `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts`, and `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` route through `composeTaskRequest(...)` before `createTask(...)`, while `src/shared/lib/tasks/videoEnhance.ts` calls `createTask(...)` directly with an inline payload (lines 232-249).
This creates two effective integration contracts with task creation behavior (composed vs direct), increasing drift risk when lineage/default/normalization behavior in request composition changes.
dependency_health.issues_preventing_higher_score: Task modules rely on inconsistent dependency paths to the same task-creation boundary, so changes to request-shaping behavior are not uniformly enforced.

evidence: High-risk modules in scope have direct tests (for example: useTaskStatusCounts, useLineageChain, generationTaskRepository, generationTaskCache, generationTransformers, timelineWriteQueue, referenceDomainService, generateVideoService, edgeRequest, rateLimit, orchestrator), but smaller boundary helpers in this batch have no direct test files.

normalizeTaskDetailsPayload contains URL-cleaning and fallback selection logic (`cleanUrl`, `pickTaskDetailsString`) that can silently alter UI payload rendering but has no colocated test coverage in this batch.
safePlay wraps browser autoplay behavior and converts exceptions into OperationResult contracts, but there is no direct test coverage in this batch for rejection/Abort-like branches.
test_strategy.issues_preventing_higher_score: Critical paths are mostly tested, but untested normalization/media-boundary helpers still leave regressions possible in user-visible details and playback error handling.

evidence: Travel request contract declares `enhanced_prompts?: string[]` in `TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams`, but request-building path uses `Array<string | null>` (`PairConfigPayload.enhancedPromptsArray`) and forwards it directly when any entry is non-empty.

Non-2xx cost-calculation parsing only checks `errorCode` while the same module’s success-path parser accepts both `errorCode` and legacy `error`, creating split semantics for the same upstream API family.

`src/shared/lib/taskTypeUtils.ts`, `src/shared/lib/galleryUtils.ts`, `src/shared/lib/timelinePositionCalculator.ts`, and `src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts` contain extensive explanatory block comments that restate obvious code mechanics and repeated 'single source of truth/canonical' phrasing.

`src/shared/lib/timelineNormalization.ts` and `src/shared/lib/uiSelectors.ts` currently carry header-style commentary with minimal or no substantive implementation, indicating template/comment residue outweighing code signal.
`src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts` adds proxy-based warned aliases and an extra wrapper for `travelShotUrl`, increasing ceremony for a module explicitly marked deprecated.
ai_generated_debt.issues_preventing_higher_score: Comment and wrapper ceremony is repeated across multiple utility modules, reducing signal-to-noise and making migration-state boundaries harder to read quickly.

evidence: Deprecated shim modules `src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` and `src/shared/hooks/useDerivedItems.ts` remain in tree while no non-self imports were found, indicating migration completed but compatibility files not retired.

`src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts` is deprecated, but references remain across tests, and the shim still exports behavior through runtime Proxy warnings instead of being removed and test imports updated.
`src/shared/lib/tasks/legacy/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` exports a deprecated migration adapter with no non-test callers.

Travel-between-images still carries dual modern+legacy contracts across `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts` and `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts`; `payloadBuilder.ts` still accepts `legacyStructureCompatibility` even though canonical creation path does not pass it.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` keeps deprecated `handleError`/`handleAndRethrow` exports while broad production usage still targets those names, so migration to `normalizeAndPresent*` is incomplete.

`src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` and `src/shared/hooks/useDerivedItems.ts` are deprecated compatibility shims that only re-export domain hooks, leaving legacy entrypoints in a broad shared package instead of the owning domain package.
Repository imports now target domain locations (`@/domains/generation/hooks/...`) while the shared-hook shim files remain in place, indicating migration residue and blurred package boundaries.

`src/shared/hooks/useEditToolMediaPersistence.ts` is edit-media/edit-video specific (tool settings + generations fetch) but sits in the top-level `src/shared/hooks/` bucket; its test is also in the flat `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/` root.

Holistic structure shows flat overload in `src/shared/lib/` (70 production files) and the assigned files there (`dragDrop.ts`, `dragDropUpload.ts`, `dummyNames.ts`) span unrelated concerns, reducing navigability and ownership clarity.

Controller/orchestrator hooks in this batch carry multiple unrelated concerns: UI state, async workflow orchestration, persistence/network side effects, and cross-module coordination in single modules (for example `useMediaGalleryActions`, `useTimelineOrchestrator`, `useSegmentSlotMode`, `useTasksPaneController`).

Task lifecycle modules combine policy and transport logic in one place: `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` handles segment-completion gating, status transitions, billing decisioning, and billing metadata persistence fallback paths.

Wide parameter/threading surfaces increase coupling: `useFormSubmission` and `useSubmissionOrchestrator` pass large prop bags with overlapping fields, and `promptManagement/types.ts` / `referenceManagement/types.ts` expose very broad input/output interfaces that force cross-feature awareness.
supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts enforces authenticateRequest(...) and explicitly rejects non-service-role callers (`!auth.isServiceRole` -> 403).
supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts and supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts assert auth context at entry (`assertCompletionAuthContext(...)`) before mutation flows.
Client-side mutations in src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts add project-scope verification before updates/deletes (verifyGenerationScope/verifyVariantScope/verifyProjectScope).
authorization_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Authorization checks are split across multiple patterns (service-role guard, auth-context assertions, and ad-hoc scope verification), which increases audit surface and drift risk over time.

evidence: src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts marks `handleError`/`handleAndRethrow` as deprecated with a removal target, but active callsites still use them (e.g. src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts, src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts, src/features/gallery/lib/adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.ts).

Sibling modules already use newer naming and contracts (`normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow` in src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts, `reportError/reportAndRethrow` in src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts), creating mixed conventions inside the same runtime layer.
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: Error API naming and migration conventions are not uniformly applied, so callers in adjacent modules follow different standards for equivalent behavior.

evidence: Error handling behavior is fragmented: some paths normalize/present (`handleError` in useDiscordSignIn/useTaskStatusCounts), others use core reporter (`reportError` in useMediaGalleryHandlers), and others return operation envelopes (`safePlay` returns operationFailure without throwing).
Deprecated adapters in src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts still front active flows, while newer flows bypass them directly, producing inconsistent error semantics and migration state.

In task-fetching hooks (src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts, src/shared/hooks/useTaskStatusCounts.ts), errors are suppressed from UI (`showToast: false`) but still rethrown/logged differently than UI handlers.
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: The codebase lacks one canonical error contract at hook/component boundaries (throw vs report-only vs operation result), causing inconsistent user feedback and observability.

evidence: src/features/gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts sets `totalPages = Infinity` when `totalItems` is unknown.

src/features/gallery/lib/adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.ts computes `hasNextPage = currentPage < totalPages`, which is always true when totalPages is Infinity, so next-page prefetch is unbounded by known pagination limits.

The same coordinator catches prefetch errors and routes them to non-toast logging (`showToast: false`), so repeated over-prefetch behavior can stay silent to users while still generating avoidable load.
logic_clarity.issues_preventing_higher_score: Pagination intent is implicit and split between hook defaults and coordinator checks, making terminal-page behavior hard to reason about and easy to regress.

evidence: src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts reads `parsed_resolution_wh` via `(params as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)...`, bypassing its declared `IndividualTravelSegmentParams` interface.
The same file writes `parsed_resolution_wh` into payloads in multiple branches, indicating the field is part of real contract but omitted from the interface.
supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts casts parsed task params to `TaskCostParams` (`params as TaskCostParams`) without validating shape/type of numeric fields before cost math.
type_safety.issues_preventing_higher_score: Runtime payload contracts and TypeScript interfaces are diverging in task-related modules, and unsafe casts are being used to bridge the gap instead of formalizing the shared types.

findings.summary: Error-reporting contracts are fragmented across facade, core, and presentation modules, weakening a single architectural boundary.
evidence: `handleError.ts` defines canonical wrappers and deprecates legacy names (`normalizeAndPresentError`, `reportRuntimeError`, `handleError`) but still exports all variants (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts:15-45`).
`useMediaGalleryHandlers` bypasses the facade and imports `coreReporter` directly (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:3`).
`useMediaGalleryItemState` bypasses both and imports `presentationReporter` directly (`src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts:2`).
findings.suggestion: Enforce one public error-reporting entrypoint (for example `@/shared/lib/errorHandling`) and block deep reporter imports with lint boundaries; migrate callers to the single contract and remove deprecated shims on schedule.

findings.summary: Toast runtime relies on import-time singleton initialization, coupling unrelated modules to global mutable state.
evidence: `toastManager` is instantiated at module load (`src/shared/components/ui/toast.tsx:11`) rather than via explicit runtime bootstrap.

`sonner.tsx` imports and re-exports `toast` and injects `toastManager` into provider (`src/shared/components/ui/runtime/sonner.tsx:2,25-33`), so consumers can mutate toast state without owning provider lifecycle.
findings.suggestion: Move toast-manager creation behind an explicit initializer/provider-owned factory and expose access through context or an injected runtime service; keep a thin compatibility layer only during migration.

findings.summary: UI-layer modules are accumulating cross-domain orchestration responsibilities, increasing coupling and change blast radius.
evidence: `ProjectContext` directly binds auth state, user settings, project CRUD, project selection, and side-effect defaults in one provider (`src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx:4-8,41-66`).

`useMediaGalleryHandlers` accepts a wide cross-cutting argument set and drives navigation, filtering, modal transitions, and external context cleanup (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:16-44,94-123`).
`LightboxShell` mixes layout-contract handling and dialog-event control logic via multiple imported subsystems (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx:21-25,67-91`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce feature-scoped application services (project session coordinator, gallery navigation coordinator, lightbox runtime controller) with narrower interfaces so UI components consume focused adapters instead of orchestrating cross-module workflows directly.

findings.summary: Task builders use oversized mixed-responsibility contracts that encode multiple layers at once
evidence: `IndividualTravelSegmentParams` carries transport, lineage, UI, migration, and worker execution data in one type (lines 36-96), then is repeatedly reshaped into different payloads (`buildSegmentState`, `buildTaskParamsPayload`, `buildIndividualSegmentParamsPayload`).

`CanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput` repeats the same pattern with mode-dependent optional fields that are later filtered by imperative checks (`normalizeJoinClipsParams`) and manually rebuilt (`buildJoinClipsPayload`).
Adjacent hooks add pass-through/compat adapters (`resolveUseSegmentSlotModeInput`, legacy adapters in `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks`) rather than converging on a single canonical contract.
findings.suggestion: Split task inputs into explicit stage contracts (UI input, canonical domain command, worker payload) and make converters one-way and typed; remove pure forwarding helpers and keep temporary legacy adapters at the boundary only. Start by extracting canonical command types for `individualTravelSegment` and `joinClips`, then have editor hooks consume those command types directly.

findings.summary: Task modules depend on two different request-construction paths for the same create API
evidence: `videoEnhance.ts` directly submits inline request objects to `createTask` (lines 242-249).
Peer modules (`imageGeneration.ts` lines 441-447, `magicEdit.ts` lines 211-217, `joinClips.ts` lines 431-437, `individualTravelSegment.ts` lines 742-747) call `composeTaskRequest(...)` first.
Because request normalization is not on a single path, dependency changes in request composition are only partially applied across task families.
findings.suggestion: Standardize all task modules on one request-construction dependency path (prefer `composeTaskRequest`) and add a shared test asserting every task family includes the same lineage/default envelope behavior.

findings.summary: Travel request builder can emit null-valued enhanced prompts against a string[] API contract.
evidence: `TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` declares `enhanced_prompts?: string[]` (`types.ts`, line 130).
`buildBatchPairConfig` creates `enhancedPromptsArray: Array(numPairs).fill(null)` (`generateVideoService.ts`, lines 364-365).

`buildTravelRequestBodyV2` forwards `enhanced_prompts: pairConfig.enhancedPromptsArray` when any prompt is non-empty (`generateVideoService.ts`, lines 491-511), so mixed arrays can include null values despite the contract type.
findings.suggestion: Normalize `enhancedPromptsArray` to a pure `string[]` before assignment (e.g., coerce null to '' or omit the field unless every element is string), and align `PairConfigPayload` typing with `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` so the compiler enforces the same shape.

findings.summary: Cost-calculation response parsing uses different error-key semantics for success vs non-success HTTP paths.
evidence: In the 2xx branch, parser treats either `errorCode` or `error` as an error payload (`billing.ts`, lines 273-276).
In the non-2xx branch, parser only reads `errorCode` and falls back to `cost_calculation_failed` (`billing.ts`, lines 323-327), ignoring `error` if that is the populated key.

Shared error envelope utilities emit both fields today (`edgeRequest.ts`, `edgeErrorResponse`), but parser asymmetry means this caller is brittle to legacy/partial envelopes and can lose specific error identity.
findings.suggestion: Use a single helper for error-code extraction in both branches (prefer `errorCode`, fallback to `error`) and apply it uniformly so billing reconciliation decisions see stable codes regardless of HTTP status.

findings.summary: Two boundary helpers in this batch lack direct tests despite behavior that can silently regress.
evidence: `normalizeTaskDetailsPayload` includes behaviorful transforms (`cleanUrl`, multi-source `pickTaskDetailsString`) that affect rendered task details (`normalizeTaskDetailsPayload.ts`, lines 21-23 and 41-47).
`safePlay` maps browser `video.play()` failures into `OperationResult` and structured error metadata (`safePlay.ts`), a branch-heavy browser boundary.
Repository test file discovery shows no direct `*.test.*` files for these two modules in the current codebase, while neighboring complex modules in this batch are directly tested.
findings.suggestion: Add focused unit tests for `normalizeTaskDetailsPayload` (quote-cleaning, source precedence, null-task path) and `safePlay` (play resolves, play rejects, error mapping fields) to close boundary regressions.

findings.summary: Deprecated generation-hook compatibility files remain after call-site migration.
evidence: Both files are pure deprecated re-export shims.
No non-self imports of `@/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` or `@/shared/hooks/useDerivedItems` were found in `src`.
findings.suggestion: Delete both shim files and keep only domain-owned hook entrypoints; if path stability is required, move aliases to a single barrel with an explicit removal deadline and CI guard for new imports.

findings.summary: Deprecated tool constants alias persists as a runtime shim despite canonical replacements.
evidence: `toolConstants.ts` is marked deprecated and wraps canonical exports with Proxy warning indirection.
Non-test production imports of `@/shared/lib/toolConstants` were not found, but tests still reference it heavily, keeping migration debt alive.
findings.suggestion: Migrate tests to `toolIds`/`toolRoutes` directly, then remove `toolConstants.ts`; if temporary retention is needed, convert it to static re-exports without Proxy behavior to reduce drift before deletion.

findings.summary: Legacy task migration adapters remain first-class exports without active runtime consumers.
evidence: `createJoinClipsTaskCompat` is explicitly deprecated and had no non-test callers.
Travel-between-images still maintains dedicated legacy field contracts and enforcement helpers in parallel with canonical structures.
findings.suggestion: Move legacy-only behavior behind explicit ingress adapters (or test fixtures), stop exporting them as normal task APIs, and schedule physical removal once migration metrics reach zero.

findings.summary: Deprecated error helper names coexist with new canonical names and remain heavily used.
evidence: `handleError.ts` marks `handleError` and `handleAndRethrow` deprecated with a removal target but still exports them.
Repository usage still shows broad production call sites to deprecated names, indicating rename migration has not converged.
findings.suggestion: Run a codemod to migrate call sites to `normalizeAndPresentError`/`normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow`, keep temporary aliases in a separate migration file, then remove deprecated exports by target date.

findings.summary: Multiple utility files show generated-style documentation ceremony that restates code.
evidence: Large introductory comment blocks and repeated in-function commentary explain straightforward operations (e.g., spacing math, category checks) without adding domain constraints.
Similar style repeats across nearby files (`galleryUtils.ts`, `typedEvents.ts`, `taskIdHelpers.ts`) with 'canonical/single source of truth' phrasing.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to intent-only notes (business constraints, invariants, migration policy), remove line-by-line narration, and enforce concise doc-comment rules for small utility modules.

findings.summary: Near-empty utility modules preserve scaffold text instead of meaningful code or deletion.
evidence: `uiSelectors.ts` contains only a comment header and no selectors.
`timelineNormalization.ts` contains a single constant plus broad normalization framing, indicating leftover scaffold naming/structure.
findings.suggestion: Either populate these files with concrete exported contract content or inline/move the single constant into the active calculator module and delete empty scaffolds.

findings.summary: Deprecated shared-hook shim files keep stale package boundaries after generation-hook migration.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` only re-exports `@/domains/generation/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` and is marked deprecated.
`src/shared/hooks/useDerivedItems.ts` only re-exports `@/domains/generation/hooks/useDerivedItems` and is marked deprecated.
Current non-test imports use domain hook paths, so the shared-package shim placement is now migration residue rather than active ownership.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) move shim consumer tests to domain-owned test locations first (or retarget imports) so coverage follows ownership; (2) update all remaining imports from `@/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` and `@/shared/hooks/useDerivedItems` to domain paths with `rg -n "@/shared/hooks/(useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm|useDerivedItems)" src`; (3) delete the two shim files once search returns no runtime consumers; (4) validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.test.ts` (or updated domain test path) and a full typecheck/lint run.

findings.summary: Edit-media persistence hook is placed in top-level shared hooks instead of media-scoped package.
evidence: The hook is explicitly for edit tool media persistence and handles edit-flow-specific settings (`settingsToolId`, `lastEditedMediaId`, editor close behavior), not generic cross-domain hook behavior.
Its test is located in flat `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/` rather than a media-scoped test folder, so package ownership is split.
The codebase already has `src/shared/hooks/media/`, indicating an existing boundary this hook should align with.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) move `src/shared/hooks/useEditToolMediaPersistence.ts` to `src/shared/hooks/media/useEditToolMediaPersistence.ts`; (2) move its test to `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/media/useEditToolMediaPersistence.test.ts` (or mirror the production folder with a colocated media test folder); (3) add a temporary re-export stub at the old path only if required for incremental migration; (4) update imports via `rg -n "@/shared/hooks/useEditToolMediaPersistence" src` and replace with the media path; (5) validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/media/useEditToolMediaPersistence.test.ts` plus `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Flat `shared/lib` placement mixes drag-drop flow code with unrelated dummy-name utility.
evidence: Holistic structure reports `src/shared/lib/` at 70 production files (well above the >10 mixed-concern threshold), indicating a flat utility bucket.

`dragDrop.ts` contains cross-component generation DnD protocol logic, `dragDropUpload.ts` is edit-flow upload handler creation, and `dummyNames.ts` is sample name generation; these are distinct ownership areas in one broad package.
The filename/function pair in `dummyNames.ts` suggests seed/mock/demo concern mixed into runtime utility namespace, increasing discovery cost.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create purpose folders under `src/shared/lib/` such as `dnd/` for drag-drop protocol (`dragDrop.ts`, `dragDropUpload.ts`) and `seeding/` or `fixtures/` for `dummyNames.ts` if it is runtime-owned; (2) move files with `git mv` in this order: drag-drop files first, then dummy-name utility; (3) update imports with `rg -n "@/shared/lib/(dragDrop|dragDropUpload|dummyNames)" src`; (4) run validation `pnpm vitest src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDrop.test.ts src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDropUpload.test.ts src/shared/lib/__tests__/dummyNames.test.ts` and project typecheck.

findings.summary: Canonical and deprecated error APIs share one hub module, weakening migration boundaries
evidence: README matrix marks deprecated aliases in `handleError.ts` with a concrete removal target (2026-06-30), but the same file is still the canonical contract entrypoint.
`handleError.ts` exports both `normalizeAndPresent*` and deprecated `handleError`/`handleAndRethrow`, so callers can keep importing legacy names from the canonical path indefinitely.
The file is a high-import hub in holistic context, so mixed canonical+legacy exports amplify blast radius and delay compatibility cleanup.
findings.suggestion: Split compatibility from canonical surface: keep `handleError.ts` exporting only canonical `normalizeAndPresentError`/`reportRuntimeError`/`normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow`, move deprecated aliases to a dedicated compat module (for example `errorHandling/compat/legacyHandleError.ts`), then route all temporary alias imports through that module and enforce a no-new-imports check on the compat path.

findings.summary: Governance policies are documented, but high-risk contract surfaces lack dedicated gate tests
evidence: README requires governance gates before merging contract changes, but assigned contract files are not equally protected by dedicated contract tests.
`handleError.ts` includes deprecation metadata and removal date, yet no file-local contract test in the errorHandling test folder verifies alias retirement progress or blocks regressions.
`cn.ts` has a contract test in the UI package, while `theme/button.tsx` is also labeled stable canonical in README without equivalent explicit contract coverage visible near the contract file.
findings.suggestion: Add governance contract tests for each canonical surface: (1) `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/__tests__/handleError.contract.test.ts` asserting canonical exports and explicit deprecation policy behavior, (2) `src/shared/components/ui/__tests__/button.contract.test.ts` asserting import path, export shape, and variant API compatibility, then include both in the contract test globs used by governance gates.

findings.summary: UI package is oversized and mixes primitives/theme/runtime concerns, obscuring ownership
evidence: Holistic structure shows `src/shared/components/ui/` at 57 files with mixed component, primitive, runtime, and test responsibilities.
`theme/button.tsx` still composes from parent-level `Slot` and `baseButton` and primitive `cn`, showing partial layering rather than clear package boundaries.
Coupling matrix shows high concentration through this package (`shared/components -> ui` and `ui -> ui/primitives`), increasing navigation and change coordination costs.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create target folders `src/shared/components/ui/contracts/`, `src/shared/components/ui/primitives/`, `src/shared/components/ui/themes/app/`, `src/shared/components/ui/runtime/`, `src/shared/components/ui/composites/`. Stage 2 move files in order: contract-entry files first (`theme/button.tsx`, `primitives/cn.ts`) to `contracts/`, then migrate implementation files (`baseButton.tsx`, `buttonThemeVariants.ts`, `Slot.tsx`) under `themes/app` and `primitives`, then move runtime presenters (`registerToastErrorPresenter.ts`, sonner runtime) into `runtime`. Stage 3 run import updates and validation with `rg -n "@/shared/components/ui/(theme/button|primitives/cn|baseButton|Slot)" src`, then `npm run test:contracts`, `npm run test:arch`, and `npm run quality:check` to verify no contract regressions.

findings.summary: Media gallery hooks combine UI behavior, backend orchestration, and transport details
evidence: `useMediaGalleryActions` contains optimistic deletion/backfill sequencing with debounced refetch, page-bounds correction, direct XHR download transport, blob/file extension handling, DOM download triggering, and tick timeout management in one hook.

`useMediaGalleryPagination` manages page derivation, server/client branching, loading state machine, timeout fallbacks, server correction notifications, and scroll behavior; this mixes pagination domain logic with view/interaction concerns.
These hooks must coordinate via shared side-effect conventions (loading clear, backfill timing), which increases hidden coupling and makes behavior hard to change safely.
findings.suggestion: Split gallery behavior into explicit layers: a pure pagination state hook, a gallery task-actions service (delete/backfill/download), and a thin UI adapter hook. Keep DOM-specific concerns (scroll/download element) in view-level utilities so data workflow and UI timing can be tested independently.

findings.summary: Timeline/segment editor hooks expose oversized orchestration surfaces
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` aggregates device detection, drag/drop, zoom, selection, pending states, endpoint dragging, structure-video browser actions, and returns a very broad nested API object.
`useSegmentSlotMode` combines routing deep-link behavior, pair mapping, frame updates, structure-video overlap resolution, and lightbox state management in one module.
The orchestration layer is absorbing both domain transforms and UI lifecycle logic, causing very large hook interfaces and high change blast radius.
findings.suggestion: Introduce feature-bound sub-hooks with strict ownership (timeline interaction state, segment-slot routing state, structure-video overlap policy, frame update policy) and compose them in a minimal coordinator that only wires outcomes together.

findings.summary: Image submission pipeline relies on wide pass-through parameter bags
evidence: `useFormSubmission` destructures and forwards a large set of fields to multiple helpers (`useTaskParamsBuilder`, `useSubmissionOrchestrator`), creating wide coupling between form state and orchestration internals.
`UseSubmissionOrchestratorInput` mirrors many `UseFormSubmissionProps` members directly and performs multiple command variants with repeated queue/validation patterns.
Prompt/reference management types define broad input/output contracts, reinforcing cross-cutting knowledge requirements across modules instead of small capability-focused contracts.
findings.suggestion: Define a narrow `SubmissionContext` domain object and command-specific input contracts (`managed`, `automated`, `queueLikeExisting`) with shared validators/executors. Reduce hook signatures to capability interfaces rather than full-form state mirrors.

findings.summary: Task/orchestrator modules mix domain policy with persistence and billing side effects
evidence: `complete_task/orchestrator.ts` contains segment gate evaluation, failure policy, status transitions, billing outcome classification, and billing metadata persistence fallback in one module.
`individualTravelSegment.ts` combines validation, legacy/orchestrator snapshot normalization, seed/model/lora resolution, and payload composition in one large flow.
`imageGeneration.ts` similarly combines validation, mode-specific filtering, resolution policy, lora/reference payload assembly, and request composition.
findings.suggestion: Separate these modules into policy-only functions (pure, testable) and side-effect executors (DB/network). Extract shared task-contract parsing/validation helpers so image/travel/orchestrator paths reuse the same composition primitives instead of per-file monoliths.

findings.summary: State controller hooks conflate UI state with persistence/query orchestration
evidence: `useTasksPaneController` owns local UI filter/page state, sessionStorage policy, query composition, optimistic cache mutation, and mutation error rollback in one hook.
`PanesContext` manages lock policy, mobile/tablet rules, persistence side effects, pane-open synchronization, and layout/selection state under one provider domain.
`useProjectCRUD` handles CRUD plus onboarding bootstrap behavior (user record creation, template copy, cleanup on failure), blending operational workflows with repository-style access.
findings.suggestion: Split controller layers into: local UI state hooks, persistence adapters, and data/mutation services. Keep contexts/providers focused on state distribution, and move setup workflows into dedicated domain services invoked explicitly by caller flows.

findings.summary: Deprecated `handleError` API remains a first-class convention in active modules, conflicting with the newer normalized error API.
evidence: handleError.ts marks `handleError` and `handleAndRethrow` deprecated with `@removalTarget 2026-06-30`.
useTasks.ts and useDiscordSignIn.ts still import deprecated functions directly.
useGenerationMutations.ts already uses `normalizeAndPresent...` naming, so sibling modules follow different conventions for the same concern.
findings.suggestion: Complete the migration by codemodding remaining imports to `normalizeAndPresentError` / `normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow`, then remove deprecated exports behind a single release gate.

findings.summary: Equivalent runtime paths use incompatible error contracts (throwing, report-only logging, and operation-result envelopes), reducing predictability.
evidence: useTasks.ts uses throw-on-error via `handleAndRethrow`.
useTaskStatusCounts.ts records partial failures and returns degraded `operationFailure` state instead of throwing.
useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts uses `reportError` / `reportAndRethrow` from a different reporter layer; safePlay.ts returns `OperationResult` with recoverable reporting.
findings.suggestion: Define one boundary contract per layer (query hooks, UI handlers, media helpers) and enforce it via shared wrappers so each layer either throws or returns `OperationResult`, but not both patterns ad hoc.

findings.summary: Adjacent page preloading treats unknown totals as infinite, making next-page prefetch effectively unbounded.
evidence: useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts sets `totalPages` to `Infinity` when `totalItems` is absent.
adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.ts checks `currentPage < totalPages`; with Infinity this is always true, so every page keeps scheduling a next-page prefetch regardless of known terminal bounds.
findings.suggestion: Represent unknown totals as `null` instead of Infinity and gate next-page prefetch on explicit `hasMore`/cursor metadata from the query response.

findings.summary: Task payload types are bypassed with unsafe casts where contract fields are used at runtime but absent or weakly validated in types.
evidence: individualTravelSegment.ts reads `parsed_resolution_wh` via `(params as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)`, indicating declared params type is incomplete for actual usage.
payloadBuilder.ts writes `parsed_resolution_wh` into orchestrator payloads, reinforcing that this field is part of the real task contract.
calculate-task-cost/index.ts force-casts parsed params to `TaskCostParams` without validating numeric field types before arithmetic.
findings.suggestion: Promote a shared canonical task param schema (including `parsed_resolution_wh`) and replace force-casts with parser-based narrowing at boundaries (client builders + edge functions).
